Learning Outcome: Explain why every person and service must have a unique, traceable identity (no shared human accounts); identify your primary identifier (corporate email) and know that an immutable Employee ID exists for system synchronization
Use corporate SSO (Google Workspace SAML/OIDC) for all supported services; explain why local app passwords are prohibited unless exempted, and that any exceptions must be vaulted and rotated

Enroll in MFA using approved factors (preferred: FIDO2/WebAuthn hardware key; backup: TOTP app); explain why SMS/voice OTP is disallowed; identify which high-risk systems require a hardware key for primary sign-in
Understand CivicActions is moving toward passwordless-by-default; until then, use passwords that are ≥14 characters, unique, and stored in an approved password manager
Protect hardware keys (keychain or secure pouch, not left in laptops while traveling); configure at least one backup factor; report loss or suspected compromise immediately to IT/Security

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Module: D - Endpoint posture and privileged access
Learning Outcome: Receive and secure two hardware keys at onboarding; understand that all authenticators and sessions are revoked at offboarding
Understand the default IdP session lifetime (20 hours) and that re-authentication is required after session expiry
Explain that managed endpoints are required for Internal/Confidential (including FCI) data access; understand BYOD limitations (MFA/communications only, under MDM/profile isolation)
Use an approved password manager for any residual app passwords or recovery codes; never share credentials; store secrets only in managed secret systems, never in code or tickets

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Module: D - Endpoint posture and privileged access
Learning Outcome: Create unique NPIs mapped to a System Owner and tracked in inventory; prefer OIDC/workload identity/short-lived tokens over long-lived keys; rotate NPI secrets at least annually and immediately on compromise; never use NPIs for interactive login

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Module: D - Endpoint posture and privileged access
Learning Outcome: Understand that authentication events, factor changes, admin elevation, break-glass usage, and NPI token use are logged and auditable; know that metrics (hardware-key coverage, time-to-revoke, anomalous login rate) are reviewed annually
Complete identity verification with PeopleOps; receive account, group/role assignment, and MFA key issuance as part of onboarding
Expect prompt access updates when duties change; privileges are minimized or removed when no longer needed

Training Name: Training 6 — Access Governance, Risk & Supplier Oversight
Learning Outcome: Understand that all access is revoked immediately on separation; authenticators are retrieved or cryptographically revoked; evidence is documented in a Jira offboarding ticket
Report immediately any suspected account compromise, phishing credential capture, or loss/theft of an MFA device; know that Security follows the IR Plan to contain, eradicate, and recover

Module: F - Governance, risk, and compliance awareness
Learning Outcome: Understand that violations can result in access revocation, disciplinary action, or contract remedies
